steps.title: Identify the right triangle and label the sides relative to angle Q
steps.content:
The right angle is at $R$. From angle $Q$'s perspective:
  • Opposite side: $SR = 18$
  • Adjacent side: $QR$ (vertical)
  • Hypotenuse: $QS$ (the side we need)

steps.title: Apply the sine ratio to find QS
steps.content:
From angle $Q$, sine relates the opposite side to the hypotenuse:
$$\sin Q = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{SR}{QS} = \frac{18}{QS}$$
Solving for $QS$ by multiplying both sides by $QS$ and dividing by $\sin Q$:
$$QS = \frac{18}{\sin Q}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
